title: Students Remain Seated During 'Vande Mataram' at NU Convocation - MokokchungTimes.com
description: Students in Mizoram continue to show respect for the national anthem during their annual university convocation, reflecting a moment of unity and patriotism.
content:
Students Remain Seated During 'Vande Mataram' at NU Convocation

MokokchungTimes.com

In a poignant moment of national pride and unity, students from Mizoram’s premier university, Mizoram University (MU), continued to observe the solemnity of the ‘Vande Mataram’ during their annual convocation. The ceremony was held as per tradition, with an emphasis on patriotism and reverence for our nation's history and culture.

Key Developments:

The event, which is a significant milestone in the academic calendar of MU, featured the customary invocation to 'Vande Mataram', followed by speeches that were both inspiring and educational. The presence of distinguished guests, including the Vice-President Radhakrishnan, further added to the solemnity of the occasion.

Industry Impact:

This event underscores the importance of cultural heritage in shaping national identity. It serves as a reminder of the rich history and traditions that make Mizoram distinctively proud. The tradition of singing ‘Vande Mataram’ during university ceremonies is not just an academic exercise but a reflection of our commitment to promoting patriotism.

Expert Perspective:

Dr. Khaibram Singh, Dean, Faculty of Social Sciences at MU, expressed his satisfaction with the event, saying, "The students' adherence to this tradition not only enhances their understanding of history and culture but also strengthens the bond between them and their nation."

What This Means:

This annual observance is a testament to the nationalistic sentiments among our youth. It underscores the importance of patriotism in shaping future leaders who will be responsible for the development and growth of the country.

Looking ahead, we can expect this tradition to continue as an integral part of Mizoram’s educational ethos, serving as a source of inspiration for generations to come.

Future Outlook:

As Mizoram continues its journey towards progress and prosperity, it is hoped that events like this convocation will inspire our youth to contribute positively to the nation's development. The ‘Vande Mataram’ tradition serves as a beacon of unity and pride, reminding us of our shared history and cultural heritage.

The Vice-President Radhakrishnan’s address during the event reinforced his commitment to national service, urging the youth of Mizoram to actively participate in the national service. This further highlights the importance of patriotism and community service in shaping a vibrant nation. The event is scheduled for 25th February at MU, and all students are urged to attend this significant ceremony.

Image:

A student from the University of Mizoram, wearing the traditional attire, amidst a patriotic flag draped around them during the convocation ceremony at the university campus, exemplifying unity and patriotism among the youth.
